task_completion.description: Verify that the USLCI database and LCIA methods have been imported into openLCA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The openLCA navigation pane on the left shows the USLCI database and LCIA methods loaded in the 'Databases' section.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least three process input parameters have uncertainty distributions defined for a coal electricity process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A process tab for 'coal electricity' (or similar) is open in openLCA, specifically showing the 'Parameters' or 'Inputs/Outputs' section where at least three parameters have an 'Uncertainty' field filled out (e.g., normal, lognormal, uniform distributions).

task_completion.description: Verify that a product system has been created for the coal electricity generation process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A product system tab is open in the main editor area for the coal electricity process, or it is clearly visible under the 'Product systems' folder in the navigation pane.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Monte Carlo simulation was performed with at least 500 iterations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Monte Carlo simulation setup dialog or results tab is visible in openLCA, explicitly showing the number of iterations set to 500 (or more) and displaying calculation progress or results.

task_completion.description: Verify that the mean GWP, standard deviation, and 95% confidence interval were exported to monte_carlo_results.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text/CSV viewer shows the file 'monte_carlo_results.csv' in the '~/LCA_Results' directory, and its contents include columns or rows for Global Warming Potential (GWP) along with its mean, standard deviation, and 95% confidence interval.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the openLCA graphical user interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the openLCA application window open with active tabs for processes, product systems, or Monte Carlo results, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: Check that the exported CSV contains actual numerical simulation data and not just hardcoded placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of monte_carlo_results.csv should show plausible numerical values (decimals/scientific notation) for the mean, standard deviation, and confidence intervals, not just the words copied from the prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the output file was saved to the exact requested directory and not a temporary or incorrect path.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (like 'cat' or 'ls') or a file explorer window should confirm the file path is exactly /home/ga/LCA_Results/monte_carlo_results.csv.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target process: coal electricity. Simulation iterations: exactly 500. Output file: /home/ga/LCA_Results/monte_carlo_results.csv. Required statistics in output: mean, standard_deviation, and confidence_interval.
